## Flow Efficiency

### Per-Story Flow Efficiency

| Story | Active Time | Total Lead Time | Efficiency |

| 3-1-2 | ~2.85h | 43.1h | 6.6% |

| 3-3-1 | ~3.60h | 41.0h | 8.8% |
| 3-3-2 | ~3.15h | 34.4h | 9.2% |
| 3-4-1 | ~1.65h | 33.1h | 5.0% |
| 3-4-2 | ~4.40h | 18.9h | 23.3% |
| 7-1-2 | ~2.10h | 16.2h | 13.0% |

### Sprint Average Flow Efficiency

```
Sprint Flow Efficiency: ~10.7% (Estimated)
Industry benchmark: 15-25% typical, >40% excellent
```

**Note:** Figures are conservative — lead time includes overnight scheduling gaps between paw pipeline sessions (2026-03-07 late work, then 2026-03-08 continuation runs, then 2026-03-09 code-review-finalize batch). Within active sessions, efficiency is estimated at 35-40%.

### Wait Time Analysis

| Wait Point | Estimated Avg Wait | % of Total Wait |
|------------|-------------------|-----------------|
| Between sessions (overnight Mar 7→8) | ~10h | ~55% |
| Between sessions (Mar 8→9 finalize batch) | ~8h | ~44% |
| Within-session step transitions | ~1-3s | <1% |

Primary wait is scheduled pipeline batching, not blocking dependencies.

| Day 0 | 0.0 | 0.0 | 0.0 |
| Day 1 | 1.57 | ~14.0 | +12.43 |
| Day 2 | 3.14 | 22.0 | +18.86 |

**Interpretation:** SPI=7.0 is an artifact of the paw automation pipeline executing the entire sprint backlog in 2 days. This is expected behavior — the 14-day sprint window accommodates human review cycles, stakeholder feedback, and real-world scheduling. The pipeline compression does not indicate overly large sprint capacity.

## Pipeline Analytics

### Step Duration Analysis

| Step | Avg Duration | Min | Max | % of Cycle |
|------|-------------|-----|-----|-----------|
| create-story | 5.9 min | 5.3 min | 6.5 min | 6.7% |
| validate-story | 2.6 min | 2.0 min | 3.4 min | 3.0% |
| atdd | 8.4 min | 4.9 min | 16.4 min | 9.6% |
| dev-story | 19.7 min | 9.7 min | 40.1 min | 22.5% |
| completeness-gate | 2.5 min | 1.1 min | 7.7 min | 2.8% |
| code-review | 18.1 min | 10.9 min | 42.4 min | 20.7% |
| code-review-qg | 17.1 min | 11.8 min | 29.1 min | 19.5% |
| code-review-analysis | 4.8 min | 1.1 min | 8.1 min | 5.5% |
| code-review-finalize | 19.1 min | 11.8 min | 30.1 min | 21.9% |

_Includes primary runs only. Retry durations excluded from averages._

### Bottleneck Identification

```
PIPELINE BOTTLENECK: dev-story / code-review-finalize (tied)
  dev-story avg: 19.7 min (22.5% of cycle)
  code-review-finalize avg: 19.1 min (21.9% of cycle)
  code-review avg: 18.1 min (20.7% of cycle)

  Impact: The review pipeline (code-review + code-review-qg + code-review-finalize)
  accounts for 62.1% of total active cycle time.
  Recommendation: Focus on reducing code-review iteration cycles.
  completeness-gate retry rate (43%) suggests upfront quality improvements
  in dev-story could eliminate retries and reduce total pipeline time.
```

### Value Stream Map

| Step | Avg Active Time | Wait After | Total |
|------|----------------|-----------|-------|
| create-story | 5.9 min | ~1s | 5.9 min |
| validate-story | 2.6 min | ~1s | 2.6 min |
| atdd | 8.4 min | ~1s | 8.4 min |
| dev-story | 19.7 min | ~1s | 19.7 min |
| completeness-gate | 2.5 min | ~1s | 2.5 min |
| code-review | 18.1 min | ~1s | 18.1 min |
| code-review-qg | 17.1 min | ~1s | 17.1 min |
| code-review-analysis | 4.8 min | ~1s | 4.8 min |
| code-review-finalize | 19.1 min | — | 19.1 min |

```
Total Active Time (avg per story): 98.2 min
Total Within-Session Wait: <1 min
Between-Session Scheduling Gap: ~18h (multi-day pipeline runs)
Value-Add Ratio (active vs lead time): ~10.7% (conservative), ~36% (active sessions only)
```

### Self-Healing Pattern Analysis

| Pattern | Count | Success Rate |
|---------|-------|-------------|
| Single retry sufficient | 3 | 100% |
| Checkpoint resume (interrupt) | 1 | 100% |
| Multiple retries needed | 0 | — |
| Unrecoverable | 0 | — |

Most common failure pattern:
```
completeness-gate → retry (same step): 3 occurrences
  3-1-2, 3-3-1, 3-3-2 — all passed on attempt 2
  Likely cause: First attempt hits edge case in new cross-platform story artifacts
```

## Token Usage & Cost Analytics

Token data is available from `step_passed` events in individual story event logs. Cache tokens (creation + read) dominate due to large context windows.

### Per-Story Token Summary

| Story | Steps | Input Tokens | Output Tokens | Cache Create | Cache Read |
|-------|-------|-------------|--------------|-------------|-----------|
| 3-1-2 | 12 | ~1,374 | ~43,929 | ~65,894 | ~366,549 |
| 3-2-1 | 12 | ~1,115 | ~56,882 | ~67,675 | ~528,417 |
| 3-3-1 | 7 | ~351 | ~34,233 | ~814,655 | ~12,432,902 |
| 3-3-2 | 12 | ~296 | ~30,706 | ~821,146 | ~7,543,082 |
| 3-4-1 | 8 | ~130 | ~5,942 | ~264,793 | ~2,218,120 |
| 3-4-2 | 12 | ~404 | ~22,509 | ~647,949 | ~7,682,568 |
| 7-1-2 | 12 | ~34,432 | ~17,849 | ~1,065,990 | ~11,250,464 |

_Input/Output are direct Claude API tokens. Cache tokens reflect context reuse efficiency._

### Bottleneck Step by Tokens

dev-story and code-review account for the majority of direct output tokens (model generation) per story.

### Cost Estimation

Based on Claude Sonnet pricing (estimated):
- Direct tokens: ~38,000 input + ~212,000 output across 7 stories
- Cache represents the majority of computational work at reduced cost
- **Estimated pipeline cost for Sprint 3: ~$8-15** (estimated; varies by exact cache hit rates and actual API pricing applied)
- **Cost per story point: ~$0.36-0.68/pt** (estimated)

_All cost figures are estimates. Actual costs depend on exact API pricing tier and cache hit ratios._

## Monte Carlo Forecast

**MONTE CARLO — INSUFFICIENT DATA**

```
Available completed sprints: 3 (minimum 3 needed, but data quality note applies)
Sprint 2 retroactive assignment inflates throughput history.
Forecast reliability: LOW at 3 sprints.

Throughput history: [6, 8, 7] stories/sprint
Points history: [18, 28, 22] pts/sprint

Simplified projection (without simulation):
  Remaining project stories: 24
  Avg throughput: 7.0 stories/sprint
  P50 projection: 24 / 7.0 = 3.4 sprints → ~Sprint 6-7 (Jul 2026)

  Remaining project points: 89 pts (174 − 85)
  Avg velocity: 22.7 pts/sprint (adjusted)
  P50 projection: 89 / 22.7 = 3.9 sprints → ~Sprint 7-8 (Aug 2026)

Note: Full Monte Carlo simulation requires 5+ sprints for statistical reliability.
Run this workflow again after Sprint 5 for meaningful forecasts.
```

## Data Quality Notes

| Metric | Quality | Note |
|--------|---------|------|
| Velocity | ACTUAL | Direct from sprint-status.yaml stories × points |
| Flow time | ACTUAL | Computed from event log timestamps |
| Flow efficiency | ESTIMATED | Active time computed from step durations; total time from event log; overnight scheduling gaps inflate denominator |
| Flow type | INFERRED | Feature/Enabler derived from epic affiliation; no explicit flow_type field in story files |
| AC% | ACTUAL | From ATDD checklist audit (sprint-health-audit) |
| Token cost | ESTIMATED | Derived from event log token fields; pricing applied as industry estimate |
| Monte Carlo | INSUFFICIENT DATA | 3 sprints available; recommend running after Sprint 5 |
| Historical SPI trend | INSUFFICIENT DATA | 1 sprint with EVM data |
| Code review active time | ESTIMATED | Timestamps in event logs for multi-session finalize runs may overlap |
